title: Client Protocol — Request/Response
description: Create a ServerProtocol class that encodes and decodes messages using a JSON-based protocol. Messages are dicts: requests have {'command': '...', 'args': {...}} and responses have {'status': 'ok' or 'error', 'data': {...}}. The protocol should prefix each JSON message with a 4-byte big-endian length header. Also create a ServerClient class that connects to the server and sends commands using the protocol. It should have connect(), send_command(command, **args), and close() methods.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 7: ServerProtocol and ServerClient
import struct
import json

assert 'ServerProtocol' in dir(), 'ServerProtocol class must be defined'
assert 'ServerClient' in dir(), 'ServerClient class must be defined'

# Test ServerProtocol.encode
assert callable(getattr(ServerProtocol, 'encode', None)), 'ServerProtocol must have encode method'
assert callable(getattr(ServerProtocol, 'decode', None)), 'ServerProtocol must have decode method'

message = {'command': 'ping', 'args': {}}
encoded = ServerProtocol.encode(message)
assert isinstance(encoded, bytes), 'encode should return bytes'

# Check length header
length = struct.unpack('!I', encoded[:4])[0]
assert length == len(encoded) - 4, 'Length header should match payload size'

# Check payload
payload = encoded[4:]
decoded = json.loads(payload)
assert decoded == message, 'Payload should be valid JSON matching the input'

# Test ServerProtocol.decode
result = ServerProtocol.decode(payload)
assert isinstance(result, dict), 'decode should return a dict'
assert result['command'] == 'ping', 'decode should reconstruct the message'

# Test round-trip
original = {'status': 'ok', 'data': {'value': 42}}
encoded = ServerProtocol.encode(original)
payload = encoded[4:]
result = ServerProtocol.decode(payload)
assert result == original, 'Round-trip encode/decode should preserve the message'

# Test ServerClient has required methods
client = ServerClient('127.0.0.1', 9999)
assert callable(getattr(client, 'connect', None)), 'ServerClient must have connect method'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'send_command', None)), 'ServerClient must have send_command method'
assert callable(getattr(client, 'close', None)), 'ServerClient must have close method'
assert client.host == '127.0.0.1', 'ServerClient should store host'
assert client.port == 9999, 'ServerClient should store port'
acceptance_criteria: ServerProtocol.encode(message) returns bytes with 4-byte length prefix + JSON payload
ServerProtocol.decode(data) parses JSON bytes back into a dict
Round-trip encode/decode preserves the original message
Length header uses big-endian unsigned int (struct '!I' format)
ServerClient stores host and port, has connect/send_command/close methods
ServerClient.send_command builds a proper request dict with command and args
hints: Use json.dumps(message).encode('utf-8') to get JSON bytes.
struct.pack('!I', len(payload)) creates a 4-byte big-endian length header.
For read_message, first recv(4) for the header, then recv(length) for the payload.
sock.sendall(data) ensures all bytes are sent, unlike sock.send(data).
